sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 3.43 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.3)

statement.latex: Let$$be an infinite cardinal number. A group$G$is said to be$$-over\-nil\-po\-tent\/ if every cyclic subgroup of$G$is a member of some ascending normal series of length less than$$reaching$G$. It is not difficult to show that the class of$$-over\-nil\-po\-tent groups is a radical class. Is it true that if$_1 < _2$for two infinite cardinal numbers$_1$and$_2$, then there exists a group$G$which is$_2...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
